faculty.name: Christos Alexopoulos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops simulation analysis methodology and applied probability models, with recent work on sequential procedures for risk-measure estimation and ML-driven optimization of production-inventory systems.

faculty.name: Turgay Ayer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Health Systems, Operations Research, Data Science
faculty.summary: Develops data-driven mathematical and computational models to support medical decision-making, inform health policy, and improve socially responsible operations, with applications in disease screening, drug pricing, and blood collection logistics.

faculty.name: Santanu Dey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Designs and evaluates algorithms for solving non-convex optimization problems, particularly mixed integer linear and nonlinear programs, with applications in power engineering, logistics, and sparse PCA.

faculty.name: Alan Erera
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on transportation and logistics systems planning and control under uncertainty, including dynamic vehicle routing for same-day distribution, resilient food supply chain design, and freight consolidation networks.

faculty.name: Nagi Gebraeel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops federated and causal representation learning methods for trustworthy decisions in distributed industrial systems, with applications in equipment prognostics, smart grid security, and sensor-driven maintenance optimization.

faculty.name: Xiaoming Huo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Statistics, High-Dimensional Data Analysis, Signal Processing
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of statistics and data science, developing algorithmic tools for high-dimensional inference, sparse representation, and multiscale analysis with applications in biomedical imaging, cybersecurity, and finance.

faculty.name: Roshan Joseph
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel data analytic methods for complex engineering problems, with contributions spanning experimental design methodology, Bayesian computation, computer experiments, and uncertainty quantification for physical systems.

faculty.name: Pinar Keskinocak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Health Systems, Humanitarian Logistics, Supply Chain
faculty.summary: Applies operations research to societal-impact problems including infectious disease spread and control, vaccine catch-up scheduling, hospital operations management, and disaster preparedness, partnering with CDC, American Red Cross, and the Carter Center.

faculty.name: Seong-Hee Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops data-based decision-making methods with theoretical rigor, focusing on stochastic simulation optimization, ranking and selection procedures, and statistical process monitoring with manufacturing applications in shipbuilding and battery systems.

faculty.name: Guanghui (George) Lan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Builds foundational optimization methods for machine learning and intelligent decision-making, with emphasis on efficient first-order and stochastic algorithms, optimization for reinforcement learning, and parameter-free adaptive methods.

faculty.name: Jing Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops ML/AI methods for complex-structured data integrating domain knowledge, with applications in precision medicine for brain conditions (cancer, Alzheimer's, TBI), manufacturing quality, and precision agriculture.

faculty.name: Siva Theja Maguluri
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Stochastic Models, Reinforcement Learning, Queueing Networks
faculty.summary: Investigates fundamental problems in stochastic systems and learning, developing algorithms for resource allocation, scheduling, and dynamic pricing in networked systems including data centers, wireless networks, and ride-hailing platforms.

faculty.name: Renato Monteiro
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on theory, complexity analysis, and implementation of algorithms for continuous optimization, including semidefinite programming, large-scale linear programming, and saddle-point/variational inequality problems.

faculty.name: Benoit Montreuil
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Supply Chain Engineering, Physical Internet, Logistics
faculty.summary: Leads the Physical Internet Initiative, researching hyperconnected logistics ecosystems that integrate systems science, optimization, simulation, and AI to enable efficient, resilient, and sustainable global supply chains and urban logistics.

faculty.name: Arkadi Nemirovski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer of modern convex optimization theory and algorithms, with foundational contributions to interior point methods, the ellipsoid algorithm, robust optimization, and complexity analysis of nonlinear convex programs with engineering and statistical applications.

faculty.name: Kamran Paynabar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops engineering-driven statistical modeling and learning methods for high-dimensional complex data including multi-stream signals, images, and point clouds, with applications in automotive, aerospace, medical devices, and public health.

faculty.name: Spyros Reveliotis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Manufacturing Automation, Discrete Event Systems, Control Theory
faculty.summary: Applies modern control theory and operations research to automated manufacturing and logistics, including deadlock avoidance in resource allocation systems, mobile robot coordination, and qubit traffic control for quantum computing.

faculty.name: Katya Scheinberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Continuous Optimization, Derivative-Free Methods, Machine Learning
faculty.summary: Develops practical algorithms and theoretical analysis for continuous optimization, with seminal contributions in derivative-free optimization and recent work on probabilistic methods and stochastic optimization for machine learning and reinforcement learning.

faculty.name: Jianjun Shi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Manufacturing Systems, Quality Engineering, Data Fusion
faculty.summary: Created the Stream of Variation (SoV) methodology for modeling and controlling variation in multistage manufacturing processes, integrating system informatics, advanced statistics, and control theory for data-driven quality improvement across automotive and aerospace industries.

faculty.name: Mohit Singh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops approximation algorithms for discrete optimization problems with applications in cloud computing, logistics, and machine learning, recognized for pioneering iterative methods in combinatorial optimization (Tucker Prize 2009).

faculty.name: Alejandro Toriello
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theory and algorithms for supply chain management and logistics, with contributions in submodular dispatching, dynamic matching for on-demand platforms, and approximation methods for vehicle routing and network problems.

faculty.name: Pascal Van Hentenryck
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer of constraint programming who fuses AI and optimization to solve large-scale societal problems in energy, supply chains, health care, and mobility, and directs the NSF AI Institute for Advances in Optimization (AI4OPT).

faculty.name: Yao Xie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theory-driven, computationally efficient methods for sequential inference with high-dimensional and spatio-temporal data, emphasizing change-point detection, uncertainty quantification, and recent integration of generative AI for robust decision-making.

faculty.name: Chuck Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Advanced Manufacturing, Composite Structures, Additive Manufacturing
faculty.summary: Researches additive and advanced manufacturing processes including 3D printing of electronics, scalable nano-manufacturing, and composite structures joining and repair, directing the NSF CHMI IUCRC for composite and hybrid material innovation.

faculty.name: Enlu Zhou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theory and algorithms for decision-making under uncertainty, connecting simulation, stochastic optimization, and control with modern ML techniques including Bayesian optimization, risk-averse reinforcement learning, and natural policy gradients.

funding.note: Fully funded (all Rossin College PhDs 100% funded)
Most doctoral students funded via research assistantships

requirements.admission_model: Direct admission to department; includes GTA component
